spec_match.rationale: Page runs script and renders dynamic UI, but the visible API surface (window., document.querySelector, revert) only partially mirrors what the feature summary calls out — the headline behaviour isn't unambiguously demonstrated.

interactive.rationale: Some controls present but the interactive surface is thin (6 buttons / 0 inputs / 0 selects). Hard to call it hands-on.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Concept lede matches the feature-index promise on key terms (design, layer, revert, system, three, unset, value, which).

edge_cases.rationale: No visible edge case / fallback / error handling — only the happy path.

references.rationale: Page does not include the chromestatus.com/feature/<id> link — violates the rule #3 self-heal invariant.

design_system.rationale: 4 raw hex colour(s) still in the style block. The design system requires CSS variables (`--bg-ivory`, `--text-charcoal` etc.)

openQuestions.title: Tighten the link between the demo and the spec's headline behaviour
openQuestions.detail: Confirm the on-screen interaction maps to a concrete API call from the feature summary on chromestatus: "The revert-rule keyword rolls back the cascade to the previous rule, similar to how revert-layer rolls back the cascade to the previous layer. For example:  ``` div { color: green; } div { color: ". If the call is being shimmed or simulated, surface that fact in the page and add a real invocation behind a feature-detect.

openQuestions.title: Expand the interactive surface beyond one or two buttons
openQuestions.detail: Add controls that vary the parameters the spec exposes — toggles, ranges, selects — so the user can drive the demo through several distinct states instead of a single one-shot trigger.

openQuestions.title: Add explicit edge case coverage
openQuestions.detail: Pick one failure mode the spec or chromestatus motivation calls out (unsupported browser, denied permission, empty input, etc.) and surface it as a distinct demo state alongside the happy path.

openQuestions.title: Add the chromestatus.com/feature/<id> link
openQuestions.detail: Page is missing the chromestatus.com/feature/5146458504429568 link inside a `references` section. CLAUDE.md rule #3 requires this on every concept so the slug-recovery script can re-derive the canonical name.

openQuestions.title: Swap raw hex colours for CSS variables
openQuestions.detail: Find the remaining hex literals in the inline `<style>` and replace each with the matching variable from `public/styles.css`. Re-check WCAG AA on any new pair you create.

openQuestions.title: Manually verify against the spec text
openQuestions.detail: Reviewer-v1-subagent based the spec_match verdict on the chromestatus summary ("The revert-rule keyword rolls back the cascade to the previous rule, similar to how revert-layer rolls back the cascade to the previous layer. For example:  ``` div { color: green; } div { color: revert-rule; /* Effectively green */ } ") because reading the full spec for every concept was out of budget. A human or follow-up agent should diff the demo's actual behaviour against https://drafts.csswg.org/css-cascade-5/#revert-rule-keyword to catch anything chromestatus glossed over.

summary: Concept ships ~1350 chars of script across 6 button(s) and 0 other control(s). hits 1 pass / 3 partial / 2 fail across the six rubric criteria.
